evals.prompt: Review a change that adds GET /api/invoices/{id}/download for authenticated users. Identify realistic security risks and validation tests.
evals.expected_output: Focuses on object-level authorization, tenant boundary evidence, sensitive document exposure, negative tests, logging, and a ship/hold-style readiness decision when appropriate.

evals.prompt: Before launch, check whether our GitHub Actions deploy job and container image handling are safe for production secrets.
evals.expected_output: Covers CI identity, secret storage, log/artifact leakage, least privilege, scan evidence, exceptions, and validation steps.

evals.prompt: Make this billing settings page feel less generic and improve the responsive layout.
evals.expected_output: Should not require security-engineer unless sensitive data/security controls are central; design-engineer is a better fit.
